messages: Now let me check what source files exist and understand the test environment:
Good - the source files and test files already exist. Let me read them all:
Now let me run the existing tests to see their status:
All 26 tests pass across both test files. Let me also verify the full test suite still passes:
There's 1 failed test suite (but 26 passed tests). Let me check what failed:

That's the e2e test (Playwright) which fails at import time — it's not a unit test issue and not in my scope. The unit tests all pass.  Now let me check the spec's test cases against the existing tests to see if there's any coverage gap. The spec calls for specific test cases - let me cross-reference:

Good - tests pass with 100% statement/line/function coverage. But comparing against the spec, I notice a few missing test cases and a branch coverage gap. Let me add:  1. **QuickViewModal**: `imageSize="sm"` prop verification (spec #12) 2. **ProductTile**: Branch coverage for `productName` fallback to `name` (line 28), and a product with only `name` set
Now add the missing branch coverage test and additional tests to the product-tile test file:
Now let me run the tests again to verify everything passes:

All 32 tests pass with **100% coverage across all metrics** (statements, branches, functions, lines). The branch coverage went from 88.88% to 100% with the added test cases.  Now let me commit and complete the pipeline:

The **storefront-unit-test** phase for **product-quick-view** is complete. Here's a summary:  ## Results  ### Test Coverage: 100% across all metrics  | File | Statements | Branches | Functions | Lines | |---|---|---|---|---| | `product-tile/index.jsx` | 100% | 100% | 100% | 100% | | `quick-view-modal/index.jsx` | 100% | 100% | 100% | 100% |  ### Tests Written: 32 total (all passing)  **QuickViewModal (`index.test.js`) — 16 tests:** - Modal shell: loading spinner, ProductView rendering, data-testid, aria-label, closed state, close button, error state - ProductView integration: product data, "View Full Details" link, Add to Cart button, `showFullLink`, `imageSize="sm"`, `isProductLoading`, full props bundle verification - Accessibility: Escape key closes modal, aria-label fallback for unnamed products  **ProductTile Override (`index.test.js`) — 16 tests:** - Overlay bar rendering: standard product, eye icon + text, aria-label - Exclusions: product sets, bundles, missing productId, undefined product - Interactions: click opens modal, preventDefault, stopPropagation, close hides modal - Visual/structural: `role="group"` for hover pseudo, item-type product support - Branch coverage: `productName` → `name` fallback, empty name fallback

items.label: e2e-author
messages: Now I have a complete understanding of the feature. Let me create the E2E test file.  **Intent: Writing E2E test file**  Key data-testid contract: - `quick-view-btn` — overlay bar/button on product tile - `quick-view-modal` — modal content container   - `quick-view-spinner` — loading spinner - `quick-view-error` — error/unavailable state
Now let me run the self-review gate to verify no banned patterns:
No banned patterns found. Let me verify the file looks correct with a quick syntax check:
Those are all `@types/node` compatibility errors, not errors in our test file. The test file itself has no TS errors. Now let me commit and complete the pipeline:

The `e2e-author` phase is complete. Here's a summary of what was delivered:  ## E2E Test File: `e2e/product-quick-view.spec.ts`  **15 tests** across 4 test groups:  ### Quick View Overlay Bar (5 tests) | Test | What it validates | |---|---| | Button appears on PLP | `data-testid="quick-view-btn"` elements exist on product tiles | | Accessible label | `aria-label` starts with "Quick View" + product name | | Contains "Quick View" text | Button textContent includes the label | | Visible on hover (desktop) | Hovering `role="group"` ancestor reveals the bar | | No PDP navigation | Clicking bar keeps URL unchanged (e.preventDefault) |  ### Modal Opening & Content (5 tests) | Test | What it validates | |---|---| | Modal opens | `data-testid="quick-view-modal"` becomes visible after click | | Spinner → content/error | Three-outcome pattern: content loads, error state, or crash detection | | Accessible aria-label | Modal has `aria-label="Quick view for {name}"` | | Product content loads | Image + interactive elements appear in modal | | View Full Details link | PDP link (`a[href*="/product/"]`) rendered via `showFullLink=true` |  ### Modal Closing (3 tests) | Test | What it validates | |---|---| | X button closes | Chakra `ModalCloseButton` → modal disappears | | Escape closes | Keyboard Escape → modal disappears | | PLP intact after close | URL unchanged, product tiles still rendered |  ### Edge Cases (2 tests) | Test | What it validates | |---|---| | Sequential opens | Open/close Quick View on multiple products without stale state | | Semantic button | `quick-view-btn` renders as `<button>` for accessibility |  **All mandatory patterns followed:** browser diagnostics capture, crash page detection, three-outcome modal assertion, `domcontentloaded` waits, no `networkidle`/`waitForTimeout`, `data-testid` selectors throughout.
